one_liner: Jira offers a mature, well-documented REST API (Cloud and Data Center) with self-serve API token or OAuth2 access on all plans including free, making it highly buildable for agent toolkits; multiple community MCP servers already exist.

auth_methods.notes: Recommended for Cloud and Data Center integrations; 3LO OAuth2 for Cloud.
Basic auth using email + API token, generated via Atlassian account settings; recommended only for scripts/bots, not production.
Personal Access Tokens (PATs) supported for Data Center instances.
JWT used for Atlassian Connect apps; OAuth 1.0a is deprecated but still available.

self_serve_notes: Full REST API access is included on Jira's free Cloud plan; developers can self-generate API tokens from account settings with no special developer license required.

api_surface.notes: Extensive REST API covering issues, projects, boards, sprints, backlogs, epics, deployments, builds, feature flags, security info, and more across both Cloud and Data Center platforms.

evidence.supports: Describes REST API authentication methods including JWT for Connect apps and overall API structure.
Details supported authentication methods for Data Center: OAuth2, PAT, OAuth1.0a, Basic HTTP.
Explains basic auth using email and API token via Base64 encoding.
Confirms REST API access is available on Jira's free plan.
Describes free Atlassian Cloud software plans including Jira.

flags: Multiple unofficial/community MCP servers exist rather than an official Atlassian-maintained MCP server
Basic auth explicitly discouraged for production use
OAuth 1.0a deprecated but still listed as an option
